channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Man Drowns in Lake While Fishing in Hazaribag
item.description: A tragic incident unfolded in Hazaribag, Jharkhand, where a man lost his life while fishing in a local lake. Details surrounding the exact circumstances of the drowning are still emerging, but authori...

item.title: Authorities identify 19-year-old woman killed in dog attack in Essex
item.description: British police have identified the young woman who tragically died following a dog attack in Essex as 19-year-old Jamie-Lea Biscoe. The incident occurred earlier this week in a residential area, promp...

item.title: NBI website and online services temporarily down amid Internet provider migration
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) announced on Sunday night that its official website, including the Online Clearance Application Portal and its email systems, is currently inaccessible due t...

item.title: Mpumalanga police launch internal and criminal probe after alleged assault by constable
item.description: Mpumalanga police have initiated a formal investigation following the emergence of a video appearing to show a constable assaulting a civilian at the Volksrust Police Station on 2 April 2026. The prob...

item.title: US-Iran talks end without agreement as tensions persist over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Historic negotiations between the United States and Iran in Pakistan concluded without a peace agreement, leaving the status of a fragile two-week ceasefire uncertain. US Vice President JD Vance stres...

item.title: Over 400 Kenyan Companies Face Dissolution for Non-Compliance or Dormancy
item.description: The Deputy Registrar of Companies in Kenya has published two official gazette notices listing over 400 companies that could soon be dissolved. The move is part of a nationwide effort to clean up the c...

item.title: Sinner downs Alcaraz in windy Monte Carlo final to secure first clay Masters and return to world number one
item.description: In Monte Carlo on Sunday, Italy’s Jannik Sinner defeated defending champion Carlos Alcaraz 7-6(5), 6-3 to win his first Masters 1000 title on clay and reclaim the world number one ranking from the Spa...

item.title: Penguins Clinch Playoff Spot, Trade Rumors and GM Dubas Receive National Recognition
item.description: The Pittsburgh Penguins secured their spot in the Stanley Cup Playoffs, offering fans relief as the NHL regular season concludes. This article highlights five key moments that shaped the Penguins' sea...

item.title: Tanzanian President receives Nasarawa State University honorary degree virtually after facing human rights criticism
item.description: Tanzanian President Samia Suluhu Hassan don cancel her planned trip come Nigeria where she suppose collect honorary doctorate from Nasarawa State University because of the wahala wey dey surround her ...

item.title: Analysis of Federal Funding Shows Mixed Results Across Ridings
item.description: Marilyn Gladu, newly elected as a Liberal MP for Sarnia-Lambton-Bkejwanong, suggested that her riding might now benefit from federal support it previously lacked while she was a Conservative. She high...

item.title: Coetzee impresses in Sekhukhune United debut against Magesi
item.description: Sekhukhune United coach Eric Tinkler expressed satisfaction with the performance of new signing Rivaldo Coetzee during his debut in the 1-0 victory over Magesi at Peter Mokaba Stadium. Coetzee, who jo...

item.title: Pope Expresses Stronger Bond with Lebanese People Amidst Regional Tensions
item.description: Pope Leo has expressed a deep and growing connection with the people of Lebanon, stating he feels 'closer than ever' to them. The statement comes during a period of ongoing instability and conflict in...

item.title: Nigerian Army confirms airstrike on Jilli terrorists, civilian casualties unreported
item.description: The Nigerian Army don confirm say dem carry out airstrike for Jilli, Yobe state wey border Borno, wey target terrorists hideout. According to Sani Uba, the Army spokesperson for Operation Hadin Kai, d...

item.title: KMD Forecasts Rain and Strong Winds in Parts of Kenya Over Next 24 Hours
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has issued a weather warning for several counties across the country. From 9 pm on Sunday, April 12, to 9 pm on Monday, April 13, rainfall is expected in regi...

item.title: Manchester City Closes Gap on Arsenal After 3-0 Win Over Chelsea
item.description: Manchester City are now giving Arsenal a proper chase in the Premier League title race after their convincing 3-0 victory over Chelsea. The game was quiet in the first half, with Chelsea looking more ...

item.title: Niagara-on-the-Lake Museum Aims to Transform Founder’s House into Research Archive
item.description: The Niagara-on-the-Lake Museum is vying for a $50,000 prize in the National Trust for Canada’s annual Next Great Save contest. The museum, located in Ontario, is one of twelve historic sites across th...

item.title: Rider killed in accident during Bob Smith Spring Cup race at Oliver's Mount, Scarborough
item.description: A motorcyclist in his thirties has tragically died following a serious crash during the Bob Smith Spring Cup at Oliver’s Mount Circuit in Scarborough, North Yorkshire. According to North Yorkshire Pol...

item.title: US-Iran Tensions Rise as IRGC Warns Against Military Vessels Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: On April 12, 2026, Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) issued a stern warning that any military vessels approaching the Strait of Hormuz would be considered a violation of the ongoing two...

item.title: Gauteng Education System Struggles with Overcrowding, Infrastructure, and Teacher Shortages
item.description: Gauteng’s education system is grappling with persistent challenges, according to Education MEC Lebogang Maile. Pupil numbers in the province have more than doubled from 1.4 million in 1995 to over 2.8...

item.title: A Barmy Army fan helps an Australian cope with extreme heat at the Ashes
item.description: During the recent Ashes Test in Adelaide, an Australian father and son made the long journey from Ballarat to witness the third Test. Upon arrival at Adelaide Oval, the oppressive 40°C heat made it al...
